user_query:
I’m funding our swim team’s training and facility upgrades and I need our scattered notes organized before my next check-in. In this workspace, there’s a small indexing script and two meeting notes under input/. Please do the following:

1) Run the indexer and fix note issues based on its output:
- Command to run: python3 input/scripts/build_summary.py --src input/notes --out workspace/notes_summary.csv
- If the script prints any errors, analyze the messages, do not change the script, and instead correct the Markdown files under input/notes so the indexer can run successfully. Rerun until it completes without errors.
- Keep the notes’ content intact; only fix structure/metadata as implied by the error messages (e.g., title heading level, ISO date format).

2) Deliverables:
- A generated CSV at workspace/notes_summary.csv including header and one row per note with columns: file,date,title,tags,open_action_items. This should be produced by the script after you fix the notes.
- A compiled action-items brief at workspace/next_meeting_action_items.md that organizes all open action items from the notes. Requirements:
  - Start with a top-level heading: "# Next Meeting Action Items".
  - Group items by owner in alphabetical order, using a level-2 heading per owner like: "## Owner: <Name>".
  - Under each owner, include only open items in the format: "- [ ] <Description> (Due: YYYY-MM-DD) — Source: <filename>".
  - Add a final section titled "## Action Item Counts by Note" listing one line per note as "<filename>: <count>", and ensure these counts match the open_action_items column from workspace/notes_summary.csv.
  - Add an appendix titled "## Troubleshooting Notes" listing the exact error messages you encountered from the indexing run(s) and a short note on how you resolved each (file edited and what was corrected).

Paths are explicit; please do not introduce additional outputs or rename files. Do not use external services; everything should run locally. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
